data.name: Abemaciclib
data.description: Investigational product for breast cancer, combined with standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

data.name: Standard Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy
data.description: Standard of care endocrine therapy of physician’s choice, given alone or in combination with abemaciclib.

data.name: Tamoxifen
data.description: An example of standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

data.name: Aromatase Inhibitor
data.description: A class of drugs used as standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

data.name: Letrozole
data.description: An example of an aromatase inhibitor.

data.name: Anastrozole
data.description: An example of an aromatase inhibitor.

data.name: Exemestane
data.description: An example of an aromatase inhibitor.

data.name: GnRH Agonist
data.description: Used for ovarian function suppression as part of standard adjuvant endocrine therapy.

data.name: Fulvestrant
data.description: Adjuvant treatment with fulvestrant is not allowed at any time during the study.

data.name: Any CDK4 and CDK6 inhibitor
data.description: Previous treatment with any CDK4 and CDK6 inhibitor is an exclusion criterion.

data.name: Exogenous reproductive hormone therapy
data.description: Concurrent use of exogenous reproductive hormone therapy (e.g., birth control pills, hormone replacement therapy) is prohibited.

data.name: Birth control pills
data.description: An example of exogenous reproductive hormone therapy.

data.name: Hormone replacement therapy
data.description: An example of exogenous reproductive hormone therapy.

data.name: Megestrol acetate
data.description: Megestrol acetate is not permitted.

data.name: Endocrine therapy for breast cancer prevention
data.description: Previous use of endocrine therapy for breast cancer prevention (e.g., tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitors) or raloxifene is an exclusion criterion.

data.name: Raloxifene
data.description: Previous use of raloxifene for breast cancer prevention is an exclusion criterion.

data.name: Other anticancer therapy
data.description: No other anticancer therapy will be permitted while patients are on study treatment, with the exception of standard endocrine therapy for breast cancer.

data.name: Strong CYP3A inhibitors
data.description: Should be substituted or avoided if possible due to potential drug interaction with abemaciclib.

data.name: Clarithromycin
data.description: An example of a strong CYP3A inhibitor that should be substituted or avoided.

data.name: Strong CYP3A inducers
data.description: Should be substituted or avoided if possible due to potential drug interaction with abemaciclib.

data.name: Rifampin
data.description: An example of a strong CYP3A inducer that should be substituted or avoided.

data.name: Substrates of P-glycoprotein, BCRP, OAT2, MATE1, MATE2-K with narrow therapeutic index
data.description: Should be substituted or avoided if possible due to potential drug interaction with abemaciclib.

data.name: Metformin
data.description: An example of a transporter substrate with narrow therapeutic index that should be substituted or avoided.

data.name: Digoxin
data.description: An example of a transporter substrate with narrow therapeutic index that should be substituted or avoided.

data.name: Dofetilide
data.description: An example of a transporter substrate with narrow therapeutic index that should be substituted or avoided.

data.name: Standard of care bone-modifying agents
data.description: Concurrent treatment with agents such as bisphosphonates and denosumab is permitted.

data.name: Bisphosphonates
data.description: An example of a standard of care bone-modifying agent.

data.name: Denosumab
data.description: An example of a standard of care bone-modifying agent.

data.name: Topical vaginal estrogen therapy
data.description: Permitted if all other non-hormonal options are exhausted.

data.name: Antiemetics
data.description: Permitted for supportive care to maximize quality of life.

data.name: Growth factors
data.description: May be administered in accordance with ASCO guidelines for neutropenia, with specific abemaciclib dose adjustments.

data.name: Antidiarrheal therapy
data.description: Should be initiated as early as possible for diarrhea management.

data.name: Loperamide
data.description: An example of antidiarrheal therapy.

data.name: Broad-spectrum antibiotics
data.description: Must be prescribed for severe diarrhea or any grade of diarrhea associated with fever or severe neutropenia.

data.name: Fluoroquinolones
data.description: An example of broad-spectrum antibiotics.

data.name: IV fluid
data.description: Administered for severe diarrhea or any grade of diarrhea associated with severe nausea or vomiting.

data.name: Electrolyte replacement
data.description: Administered for severe diarrhea or any grade of diarrhea associated with severe nausea or vomiting.

data.name: IV antibiotic therapy
data.description: Urgent initiation for febrile neutropenia.

data.name: Standard Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy
data.duration: Years 1-2, then continue to complete at least 5 years

data.name: IV antibiotic therapy for febrile neutropenia

data.description: Active ingredient in standard adjuvant endocrine therapy (aromatase inhibitor).

data.description: Class of active ingredients used for ovarian function suppression.
